subject: Spelling
read_aloud: What rhymes with cat? Cat... hat... can you think of another word that sounds like cat?
correct_response: Brilliant! That rhymes perfectly with cat! You're a rhyming superstar!
incorrect_response: Good try! Listen: cat, hat, mat - they all end with the same sound, 'at'. Can you hear it? Cat... hat... they rhyme!
hint: Think of something you wear on your head... it ends with 'at' just like cat!

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: Does 'dog' rhyme with 'log'? Do they sound the same at the end? Dog... log...
correct_response: Yes! Dog and log both end with 'og'! You've got amazing ears!
incorrect_response: Let's listen again really carefully: d-OG, l-OG. They both end with the same sound - 'og'! So yes, they do rhyme!
hint: Say them both slowly and listen to the end of each word. Dog... log... do they match?

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: What rhymes with sun? Sssuuunnn... what other word sounds like that at the end?
correct_response: That's it! It rhymes with sun! You're on a roll - this rhyming game is FUN... hey, fun rhymes with sun too!
incorrect_response: Nearly! Listen to the end of sun: 'un'. We need a word that ends with 'un' too. Sun, fun, run - can you hear it?
hint: What do you do when you go really fast with your legs? It ends with 'un' just like sun!

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: Does 'fish' rhyme with 'tree'? Fish... tree... do they sound the same at the end?
correct_response: You're right, they don't rhyme! Fish ends with 'ish' and tree ends with 'ee'. Clever listening!
incorrect_response: Let's listen carefully: f-ISH, tr-EE. 'Ish' and 'ee' sound different, don't they? So no, they don't rhyme. But you know what rhymes with fish? Dish!
hint: Say the end of each word. 'Ish'... 'ee'... do those sound the same to you?

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: What rhymes with bed? I sleep in a bed... what word sounds like bed at the end?
correct_response: Woo hoo! That rhymes with bed! They both end in 'ed'. You're a rhyme detective!
incorrect_response: Good thinking! The word bed ends with 'ed'. So we need another word ending in 'ed'. Like red! Bed, red - can you hear the rhyme?
hint: Think of a colour... it ends with 'ed' just like bed!
metacognitive_prompt: How did you think of that rhyme? Did you listen to the end of the word?

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: Does 'house' rhyme with 'mouse'? House... mouse...
correct_response: Yes! House and mouse both end with 'ouse'! A mouse in a house - that's a funny picture!
incorrect_response: Listen to the endings: h-OUSE, m-OUSE. They both end with the same sound! So yes, house and mouse do rhyme!
hint: Say them both and listen to the ending. House... mouse... do they match?

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: What rhymes with pig? A big pink pig... what word sounds like pig?
correct_response: Hooray! That rhymes with pig! A pig doing a jig in a wig - how silly!
incorrect_response: Let's try again! Pig ends with 'ig'. We need another 'ig' word. How about big? Pig, big! Or dig! Can you hear the rhyme now?
hint: Think of a word that means really large... it ends with 'ig' just like pig!

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: Does 'cake' rhyme with 'book'? Cake... book... do they sound the same at the end?
correct_response: Correct! Cake and book don't rhyme! Cake ends with 'ake' and book ends with 'ook'. You're so good at this!
incorrect_response: Listen carefully: c-AKE, b-OOK. 'Ake' and 'ook' are different sounds! So they don't rhyme. But lake rhymes with cake!
hint: Listen to the very end of each word. 'Ake'... 'ook'... are those the same sound?

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: What sound does 'snake' start with? Listen: ssssnake. What's the first sound you hear?
correct_response: Ssssuper! Snake starts with sssss! You sound just like a snake!
incorrect_response: Listen again: sssss-nake. That very first sound is 'sssss', like a snake hissing! Sssss! Can you make that sound?
hint: What sound does a snake make? Hisssss! That's the first sound in snake!

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: I spy with my little eye something beginning with 'mmm'. What sound is that? Mmmm... like mummy!
correct_response: That's the 'mmm' sound! Mmmm for mummy, mmm for milk, mmm for moon! You're brilliant!
incorrect_response: The sound is 'mmm' - press your lips together and hum: mmmmm! Like mummy, or monkey, or milk. They all start with mmm!
hint: Press your lips together and make a humming sound. Mmmmm! That's the sound!

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: What sound does 'fish' start with? Ffffffish. What's that first sound?
correct_response: Fffantastic! Fish starts with 'fff'! You bite your bottom lip and blow: ffff!
incorrect_response: Listen to the very start: ffff-ish. That 'ffff' sound is made by biting your bottom lip gently and blowing. Ffff! Try it!
hint: Bite your bottom lip gently and blow out. What sound does that make?

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: What sound does 'banana' start with? Bbb-anana. Can you hear the first sound?
correct_response: Bbb-rilliant! Banana starts with 'b'! B-b-b-banana! Well done!
incorrect_response: Listen carefully: bbb-anana. That bouncy sound at the start is 'b'. Your lips pop open: b! b! b! Like a ball bouncing!
hint: Your lips pop open for this sound. B! B! Like a bouncing ball!

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: Can you think of a word that starts with the same sound as 'tiger'? Tuh-iger. What else starts with 'tuh'?
correct_response: Yes! That starts with 'tuh' just like tiger! You're a sound-spotting champion!
incorrect_response: Let's think about the 'tuh' sound. Tiger starts with tuh. What about... table? Tuh-able! Or toy? Tuh-oy! They all start with 'tuh'!
hint: Think of your favourite plaything... it starts with 'tuh' like tiger does!

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: Do 'cat' and 'car' start with the same sound? Listen: c-at, c-ar. Same beginning or different?
correct_response: That's right! Cat and car both start with 'c' - the 'kuh' sound! Clever ears!
incorrect_response: Listen to the very start: cuh-at, cuh-ar. They both begin with the same 'cuh' sound! So yes, they start the same!
hint: Listen to just the very first sound in each word. Cuh... cuh... are they the same?

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: What sound does 'rabbit' start with? Rrrrabbit. Can you make that sound?
correct_response: Rrrright! Rabbit starts with 'rrr'! Rrrrrabbit! Like a growling lion - rrrrr!
incorrect_response: Listen: rrrr-abbit. That rumbling sound at the start is 'rrr'. It's like a little growl! Rrrrr! Try making that sound!
hint: It's a growly sort of sound. Like a little engine: rrrrrr!
metacognitive_prompt: How did you figure out that sound? Did you say the word slowly?

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: I'm thinking of an animal that starts with 'duh'. Duh... duh... it's a pet that goes woof! What is it?
correct_response: Woof woof! It's a dog! Dog starts with 'duh'! You're a sound detective!
incorrect_response: The animal that goes woof and starts with 'duh' is a dog! Duh-og! D makes the 'duh' sound!
hint: This animal loves walks, fetching sticks, and going woof! And its name starts with duh...

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: What sound does the letter S make? Can you be a slithery snake and make the S sound?
correct_response: Sssssuper! S says 'sssss' like a slithery snake! Sssss! Brilliant!
incorrect_response: The letter S makes the sound 'sssss'. Like a snake slithering through the grass! Sssss! Try hissing like a snake!
hint: Think of a snake. What sound does a snake make? Sssss! That's what the letter S says!

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: What sound does the letter A make? Not its name - its sound! Like in apple. A-a-a-apple!
correct_response: A-a-amazing! A says 'a' like in apple and ant! A-a-a! Wonderful!
incorrect_response: The letter A makes the sound 'a' - like when you open your mouth wide at the doctor's. 'Aaa!' Like in apple. A-a-apple!
hint: Open your mouth nice and wide. What do you say at the doctor's? Aaa! That's the sound A makes!

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: What sound does the letter T make? Tap your teeth together! T-t-t!
correct_response: T-t-terrific! T says 'tuh' like your tongue tapping behind your teeth! T-t-t!
incorrect_response: The letter T says 'tuh'. Put your tongue behind your top teeth and push a tiny bit of air out. Tuh! Like in tiger and top!
hint: Put your tongue behind your top teeth and give a little tap. Tuh! That's the T sound!

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: What sound does the letter P make? Pop your lips! P-p-p!
correct_response: P-p-perfect! P says 'puh' - your lips pop open! Like popping popcorn! P-p-p!
incorrect_response: The letter P says 'puh'. Press your lips together and pop them open with a puff of air. Puh! Like in pop, pig, and pan!
hint: Press your lips together and pop them open. Puh! Like a little puff! That's the P sound!

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: What letter makes the 'tuh' sound? Which letter goes tuh-tuh-tuh?
correct_response: That's right, it's T! T says 'tuh'! T for tiger, T for train! Well done!
incorrect_response: The letter that makes the 'tuh' sound is T! T-t-tiger! T-t-train! Remember, tongue behind your teeth: tuh!
hint: Think of a big stripy animal... a tiger! What letter does tiger start with?
metacognitive_prompt: How did you remember which letter makes that sound?

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: What letter makes the 'sssss' sound? Like a hissing snake - sssss! Which letter is that?
correct_response: Yesss! It's the letter S! Sssss like a snake! You remembered!
incorrect_response: The hissing snake sound 'sssss' is made by the letter S! S for snake, S for sun! Sssss!
hint: This letter looks a bit like a snake too! It's curvy and wiggly. Sssss!

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: I'm going to say a sound and you tell me a word that starts with it. Ready? Puh! What word starts with 'puh'?
correct_response: Yes! That starts with 'puh' for P! You thought of that so quickly! Star explorer!
incorrect_response: Let's think about 'puh'. Puh-puh-pig! Puh-puh-pan! Lots of words start with the 'puh' sound! Can you try one?
hint: Think of a round-faced black and white bear that eats bamboo... puh-puh-panda!

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: Let's play a matching game! Which of these starts with the 'a' sound: banana, apple, or carrot?
correct_response: Crunch! It's apple! A-a-apple! The 'a' sound is right at the start! Yummy!
incorrect_response: Listen carefully: a-a-apple! Apple starts with the 'a' sound! Banana starts with 'b' and carrot starts with 'c'. A is for apple!
hint: It's a fruit that can be red or green and is very crunchy! A-a-a...

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: What sound does the letter I make? Not its name - its short sound! Like in igloo. I-i-igloo!
correct_response: I-i-incredible! I says 'ih' like in igloo and insect! You've got it!
incorrect_response: The letter I makes the short sound 'ih' - like the start of igloo! I-i-igloo! It's a quick little sound. Ih! Ih!
hint: Think of a house made of ice... an igloo! What sound do you hear right at the start? Ih!

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: What sound does the letter N make? Touch your nose! N-n-nose!
correct_response: N-n-nice! N says 'nnn' and your tongue goes behind your top teeth! N for nose!
incorrect_response: The letter N says 'nnn'. Put your tongue behind your top teeth and hum through your nose: nnn! Like in nose and net!
hint: Put your tongue behind your top teeth and hum. Nnn! It tickles your nose! That's the N sound!

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: What sound does the letter M make? Press your lips together and hum! Mmmmm!
correct_response: Mmm-marvellous! M says 'mmm' like you're eating something yummy! Mmmmm!
incorrect_response: The letter M says 'mmm'. Press your lips together and hum like you're tasting something delicious. Mmmmm! M for mummy and milk!
hint: Press your lips together tight and hum like you just tasted something yummy. Mmmmm!

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: What sound does the letter D make? Beat a drum! D-d-d! Like a drum going d-d-d!
correct_response: D-d-dazzling! D says 'duh' like a beating drum! D-d-d-drum! Fantastic!
incorrect_response: The letter D says 'duh'. Put your tongue behind your top teeth and give it a little voice. Duh! Like a drum! D-d-drum!
hint: Pretend you're beating a drum. D-d-d! What sound are you making? That's the letter D!

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: What letter makes the 'nnn' sound? Which letter hums through your nose? Nnn!
correct_response: Yes! It's the letter N! Nnn! N for net, N for night! Brilliant!
incorrect_response: The letter that makes the 'nnn' sound is N! Think of a net, or night, or nose - they all start with N! Nnn!
hint: This letter starts the word 'nose' and 'night'. Nnn...

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: What letter makes the 'duh' sound? Like a drum beating! D-d-d! Which letter is that?
correct_response: Drumroll please! It's D! D says 'duh'! D for dog, D for dinosaur! Amazing!
incorrect_response: The drumming sound 'duh' is the letter D! D-d-dog! D-d-dinosaur! Beat that drum: d-d-d!
hint: This letter starts the word 'dog' and 'dinosaur'. Duh-duh-duh...
metacognitive_prompt: What helps you remember the D sound? Is it the drum?

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: I'm going to say a sound and you tell me a word that starts with it. Ready? Mmm! What word starts with 'mmm'?
correct_response: Mmmm-agnificent! That starts with the 'mmm' sound! You're so clever!
incorrect_response: Let's think about 'mmm'. Mmm-mummy! Mmm-milk! Mmm-monkey! Lots of words start with mmm. Can you think of one?
hint: Think of someone who looks after you and loves you lots... mmmm-mummy!

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: Which of these starts with the 'ih' sound: elephant, insect, or orange?
correct_response: Buzzzz! It's insect! Ih-ih-insect! The 'ih' sound is right at the start! Well spotted!
incorrect_response: Listen: ih-ih-insect! Insect starts with the 'ih' sound, which is the letter I! Elephant starts with 'eh' and orange starts with 'oh'.
hint: It's a tiny little creepy-crawly creature! Ih-ih-ih...

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: What sound does the letter G make? It's a gulpy sound! G-g-g! Like a drink going down - gulp!
correct_response: G-g-great! G says 'guh' like gulp and go and green! Wonderful!
incorrect_response: The letter G says 'guh'. It comes from the back of your throat, like a little gulp! Guh! G for green, G for go!
hint: Think of the colour of grass... guh-guh-green! That gulpy sound is G!

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: What sound does the letter O make? Open your mouth in a round circle! O-o-octopus!
correct_response: O-o-outstanding! O says 'o' like in octopus and orange! Your mouth makes a lovely round O shape!
incorrect_response: The letter O says 'o' - make your mouth into a round circle shape. O! Like in octopus! O-o-octopus! Eight wiggly legs!
hint: Make your mouth into a big round circle. What sound comes out? O! Like orange!

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: What sound does the letter C make? It's like a little cough! C-c-c! Like a cat coughing!
correct_response: C-c-correct! C says 'cuh' like in cat and cup! Clever you!
incorrect_response: The letter C says 'cuh' - like a little cough at the back of your throat. Cuh! C for cat, C for cup, C for cake!
hint: Think of a furry pet that says meow... cuh-cuh-cat! That coughing sound is C!

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: What sound does the letter K make? It's the same sound as C! Kuh! Like in kite and king!
correct_response: Kuh-kuh-king! That's right! K says 'kuh' just like C does! They're sound twins! Amazing!
incorrect_response: K says 'kuh' - the same sound as C! They're tricky twins! K for kite, K for king, K for kick! Kuh-kuh-kuh!
hint: It's the same sound as C! Think of flying a kuh-kuh-kite! That's the K sound!
metacognitive_prompt: Did you notice that C and K make the same sound? That's a great thing to spot!

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: What letter makes the 'guh' sound? It's a gulpy sound from the back of your throat. Guh! Which letter is that?
correct_response: G-g-gorgeous! It's the letter G! G says guh! G for gorilla! Well done!
incorrect_response: The gulpy 'guh' sound is the letter G! G for gorilla, G for garden, G for grapes! Guh-guh-guh!
hint: This letter starts the word 'garden' and 'grapes'. It's a gulpy sound... guh!

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: Which of these words starts with the 'o' sound: umbrella, octopus, or elephant?
correct_response: It's octopus! O-o-octopus with eight wiggly arms! The 'o' sound is right at the start!
incorrect_response: Listen: o-o-octopus! Octopus starts with 'o'! Umbrella starts with 'uh' and elephant starts with 'eh'. O is for octopus!
hint: It's a sea creature with eight long tentacles! O-o-o...

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: I'm going to talk like a robot! Ready? C... a... t. C-a-t. What word is that? What did the robot say?
correct_response: Cat! You understood the robot! C-a-t makes cat! You're a brilliant robot translator!
incorrect_response: Let's try again slowly. The robot said: c... a... t. Now push those sounds together faster: c-a-t... cat! It's cat! A furry pet that says meow!
hint: Push the sounds together: cuh-aaa-tuh... faster... faster... it's a furry animal that says meow!

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: Robot talk again! D... o... g. D-o-g. Can you blend those sounds together? What word is the robot saying?
correct_response: Woof! It's dog! D-o-g makes dog! You're amazing at robot talk!
incorrect_response: Let me help! The robot said: duh... o... guh. Push them together: d-o-g... dog! It goes woof!
hint: Squish the sounds together quickly: duh-o-guh... it's an animal that goes woof!

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: The robot is back! S... i... t. S-i-t. Squish those sounds together. What word did the robot make?
correct_response: Sit! That's it! S-i-t makes sit! Like sit down! You cracked the robot code!
incorrect_response: The robot said: sss... ih... tuh. Push them together really fast: s-i-t... sit! Like when you sit on a chair!
hint: Sss-ih-tuh... what do you do on a chair? You... s-i-t!
metacognitive_prompt: How did you work that out? Did you push the sounds together quickly?

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: Robot time! P... i... n. P-i-n. What word is the robot trying to say? Push those sounds together!
correct_response: Pin! You got it! P-i-n makes pin! Like a sharp little pin! Brilliant blending!
incorrect_response: The robot said: puh... ih... nnn. Faster now: p-i-n... pin! A tiny sharp pointy thing!
hint: Puh-ih-nnn... it's a tiny sharp pointy thing you use to stick things together!

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: More robot talk! M... a... p. M-a-p. What's the robot saying? Blend those sounds!
correct_response: Map! M-a-p makes map! A map shows you where to go! You're a superstar blender!
incorrect_response: The robot said: mmm... a... puh. Smoosh them together: m-a-p... map! You use it to find your way somewhere!
hint: Mmm-a-puh... you use this to find where places are and not get lost!

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: Here comes the robot! T... a... p. T-a-p. Push the sounds together. What word is that?
correct_response: Tap! T-a-p is tap! Like tap-tap-tapping! Wonderful blending!
incorrect_response: The robot said: tuh... a... puh. Faster: t-a-p... tap! Like when you tap on a door - tap tap tap!
hint: Tuh-a-puh... what do you do when you knock gently? You tap! Tap-tap-tap!

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: The robot says: S... u... n. S-u-n. Can you work out what the robot is saying?
correct_response: Sun! S-u-n makes sun! Bright and shiny like you! You're getting so good at this!
incorrect_response: The robot said: sss... uh... nnn. Push them together: s-u-n... sun! The big bright thing in the sky!
hint: Sss-uh-nnn... it's big, bright, yellow, and up in the sky!

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: Last robot word! N... e... t. N-e-t. What's the robot saying? Squish those sounds together!
correct_response: Net! N-e-t makes net! Like a fishing net or a goal net! You're a champion robot translator!
incorrect_response: The robot said: nnn... eh... tuh. Push them together: n-e-t... net! You catch things in a net!
hint: Nnn-eh-tuh... you use this to catch fish or it's on a football goal!
metacognitive_prompt: What trick do you use to blend sounds? Do you say them faster and faster?

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: Let's clap words! How many claps in 'elephant'? Ready? Clap with me! El-e-phant! How many claps was that?
correct_response: Three claps! El (clap) e (clap) phant (clap)! Three! You're a clapping champion!
incorrect_response: Let's try again together! El - that's one clap. E - that's two claps. Phant - that's three claps! Elephant has three claps! El-e-phant!
hint: Clap every time your mouth opens wide for a new beat. El... e... phant. Count those claps!

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: Clap the word 'banana'! Ba-na-na! How many claps? Clap along with me!
correct_response: Three claps! Ba (clap) na (clap) na (clap)! Banana has three beats! Brilliant clapping!
incorrect_response: Let's clap together! Ba - clap! Na - clap! Na - clap! That's three claps! Ba-na-na! Banana is a three-clap word!
hint: Clap for each chunk: ba... na... na. How many times did your hands go together?

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: How many claps in 'dog'? Just one word: dog! Clap it out!
correct_response: Just one clap! Dog! One short little clap! Some words are just one beat!
incorrect_response: Dog is just one clap! Dog! It's one short beat. Not all words have lots of claps - some are just one! Dog! Clap!
hint: It's a really short word. Say it and clap: dog! How many claps did you do?
metacognitive_prompt: Did you notice that short words often have just one clap?

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: Let's clap 'butterfly'! But-ter-fly! How many claps is that? Clap it out!
correct_response: Three claps! But (clap) ter (clap) fly (clap)! Beautiful butterfly has three beats! Lovely!
incorrect_response: Let me help! But - clap! Ter - clap! Fly - clap! Three claps! But-ter-fly! Three beautiful beats!
hint: Break it into chunks: but... ter... fly. Clap for each chunk!

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: How many claps in 'tiger'? Ti-ger! Clap with me!
correct_response: Two claps! Ti (clap) ger (clap)! Tiger has two beats! Roar! Well done!
incorrect_response: Listen and clap: Ti - clap! Ger - clap! That's two claps! Ti-ger! Two beats!
hint: Say it slowly: ti... ger. That's two chunks, so two claps!

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: How many claps in 'caterpillar'? Cat-er-pill-ar! That's a big word! Clap it!
correct_response: Four claps! Cat (clap) er (clap) pill (clap) ar (clap)! That's the biggest clapping word yet! Star explorer!
incorrect_response: It's a big word! Let's go slowly: Cat - clap! Er - clap! Pill - clap! Ar - clap! Four claps! Cat-er-pill-ar!
hint: This is a long word with lots of beats. Cat... er... pill... ar. Count each clap carefully!
metacognitive_prompt: Did you notice that long words have more claps than short words?

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: Here's a tricky word! This word is 'the'. T-H-E. The. It's tricky because it doesn't follow the normal rules. Can you say 'the'?
correct_response: That's it! 'The'! It's a word we use ALL the time. The cat, the dog, the house. You'll see it everywhere now!
incorrect_response: This word is 'the' - T, H, E. We say it 'thuh' or 'thee'. It's one of the most common words ever! The cat. The dog. The sun. It's everywhere!
hint: It's a tiny word with just three letters: T, H, E. We use it in almost every sentence! The cat sat on the mat.

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: Another tricky word! This word is 'I'. Just one letter! When you talk about yourself, you say 'I'. Like 'I am Jesse.' Can you tell me the letter?
correct_response: Yes! Just the letter I! And it's always a capital letter when it means you! I am clever! I am brilliant!
incorrect_response: The word is just the letter I! One single letter. And it's always a BIG letter, a capital I! I am happy. I am playing. I like cake!
hint: It's just one letter! When you say 'I am four years old', that word 'I' is just one letter!

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: Tricky word time! This word is 'no'. N-O. No! Like when you shake your head. What two letters spell 'no'?
correct_response: N-O spells no! That's right! It's a little word but you hear it a lot! No no no!
incorrect_response: The word 'no' is spelled N-O. Just two letters! N and O. No! Like when someone asks 'Do you want broccoli?' and you say... NO!
hint: It starts with the 'nnn' sound and ends with the 'oh' sound. Two letters! Nnn... oh...

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: Last tricky word! This word is 'go'. G-O. Go! Like ready, steady, go! What two letters spell 'go'?
correct_response: G-O spells go! Ready, steady, GO! You've learned your tricky words! Superstar!
incorrect_response: The word 'go' is spelled G-O! Just two letters. G and O. Ready, steady, go! G-O! Like 'Go to the park!' or 'Let's go!'
hint: It starts with the 'guh' sound and ends with 'oh'. Two letters, just like 'no'! Guh... oh...
metacognitive_prompt: Can you see that 'no' and 'go' look similar? They both end in O!

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: Something magic happens when S and H get together! They make a brand new sound: SHHHH! Like you're telling someone to be quiet! Can you make the 'sh' sound?
correct_response: Shhhh! Perfect! When S and H are best friends, they say 'sh' together! Like in shoe, ship, and sheep! Shhh!
incorrect_response: Put your finger on your lips like this: SHHH! That's the sound S and H make when they're together! Not 'suh' and 'huh' separately - together they say 'shhh'! Like in shop!
hint: What do you say when you want someone to be really quiet? Put your finger on your lips... shhhhh!

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: Now C and H are best friends! Together they make: CH! Like in cheese and chocolate! CH-CH-cheese! Can you make the 'ch' sound?
correct_response: Ch-ch-champion! That's the 'ch' sound! Like a train going ch-ch-ch-ch! Cheese, chips, and chocolate all start with ch!
incorrect_response: The 'ch' sound is like a little sneeze or a train: ch! ch! ch! C and H together make 'ch'. Like in cheese! Yummy ch-ch-cheese!
hint: Think of a train chugging along: ch-ch-ch-ch! Or think of something yummy and yellow on toast: ch-ch-cheese!

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: T and H are best friends too! Together they make the 'th' sound. Stick your tongue out a tiny bit and blow: thhhh! Like in 'the' and 'that'. Can you do it?
correct_response: Th-th-that's it! Tongue between your teeth and blow! Th! Like in think, this, and three! You're a digraph detective!
incorrect_response: The 'th' sound is special - you need to poke your tongue out just a little bit between your teeth and blow gently. Th! Like in 'the' and 'this'. Try poking your tongue out!
hint: Poke your tongue out just a tiny bit between your teeth and blow gently. Thhhhh! That's the sound!
metacognitive_prompt: Did you notice these special sounds are made by TWO letters working together? Clever!

subject: Spelling
read_aloud: Which special sound do you hear at the start of 'ship'? Is it 'ch', 'sh', or 'th'? Listen: shhhh-ip!
correct_response: It's 'sh'! Sh-sh-ship! The quiet sound - shhh! Like the ship is sailing quietly across the sea! Brilliant listening!
incorrect_response: Listen to the beginning: shhhh-ip. That's the 'sh' sound! Not 'ch' like cheese, and not 'th' like this. It's 'sh' like shhh, be quiet! Ship starts with sh!
hint: It's the quiet sound - the one where you put your finger on your lips. Shhhh...
